entries.definition:
كتاب الحاء

حضي

(حضر) الحاء والضاد والراء إيراد الشيء، ووروده ومشاهدته. وقد يجيء ما يبعد عن هذا وإن كان الأصل واحداً.

h–ḍ–r urban areas, a community settled around a water
source; to be present, to be at home, adjacency to water; to bring,
to fetch; to lie down dying, hardship, strife. Of this root, nine
forms occur 25 times in the Qurʾan: حَضَرَ ḥaḍara seven times;

217
entries.definition_english:
h–ḍ–r urban areas, a community settled around a water
source; to be present, to be at home, adjacency to water; to bring,
to fetch; to lie down dying, hardship, strife. Of this root, nine
forms occur 25 times in the Qurʾan: حَضَرَ ḥaḍara seven times;

217

entries.definition:
أَحْضَرَ ʾaḥḍara twice; أُحْضِرَ ʾuḥḍira once; حَاضِر ḥāḍir once;
حَاضِرِي ḥāḍirī once; حَاضِرَة ḥāḍiratun twice; مُحْضَر muḥḍar once;
مُحْضَرُون muḥḍarūn nine times and مُحْتَضَر muḥtaḍar once.

حَضَرَ ḥaḍara u [v. trans.] 1 to attend, to witness, to be present
at (4:8) وَإِذَا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ أُولُو الْقُرْبَى وَالْيَتَامَى وَالْمَسَاكِينُ فَارْزُقُوهُمْ مِنْهُ and if
[other] kinsmen, orphans or the poor attend the distribution,
make provision for them out of it [the estate] 2 to approach, to
come to (2:133) أَمْ كُنْتُمْ شُهَدَاءَ إِذْ حَضَرَ يَعْقُوبَ الْمَوْتُ were you there to
bear witness when death came to Jacob?

أَحْضَرَ ʾaḥḍara [v. IV, trans.] 1 to bring, to cause to be present
(19:68) فَوَرَبِّكَ لَنَحْشُرَنَّهُمْ وَالشَّيَاطِينَ ثُمَّ لَنُحْضِرَنَّهُمْ حَوْلَ جَهَنَّمَ جِثِيًّا so by your
Lord We will gather them together with the devils, and set them
around Hell kneeling 2 to put forward, to bring about, to submit
(81:12–13) وَإِذَا الْجَحِيمُ سُعِّرَتْ وَإِذَا الْجَنَّةُ أُزْلِفَتْ عَلِمَتْ نَفْسٌ مَا أَحْضَرَتْ and when Paradise is
brought near; then every soul will know what it has brought
about.

أُحْضِرَ ʾuḥḍira [pass. of doubly trans. v. IV] to be brought
*(4:128) وَأُحْضِرَتِ الْأَنْفُسُ الشُّحَّ human souls are ever prone to
stinginess [lit. stinginess is brought close to souls].

حَاضِر ḥāḍir [act. part.; pl. حَاضِرُون ḥāḍirūn; fem.
حَاضِرَة ḥāḍiratun] 1 present, there close at hand (18:49) وَوَجَدُوا مَا عَمِلُوا
حَاضِرًا and they will find what they did, there [in front of them] 2
ready, current, in hand (2:283) إِلَّا أَنْ تَكُونَ تِجَارَةً حَاضِرَةً تُدِيرُونَهَا بَيْنَكُمْ
but if the merchandise is there and you
hand it over, there is no blame on you if you do not write it down
3 residing at, a resident of (2:196) ذَلِكَ لِمَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ أَهْلُهُ حَاضِرِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ
this applies to those whose family is not residing near the Sacred
Mosque 4 adjacent to, bordering on, by (7:163) وَاسْأَلْهُمْ عَنِ الْقَرْيَةِ الَّتِي
كَانَتْ حَاضِرَةَ الْبَحْرِ and ask them about the township
by the sea; how its people used to break the Sabbath.

مُحْضَر muḥḍar [pass. part.; pl. مُحْضَرُون muḥḍarūn] 1 that which
is brought (3:30) يَوْمَ تَجِدُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَا عَمِلَتْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ مُحْضَرًا on the Day
when every soul finds all the good it has done brought [before it]
2 brought there, made ready (36:75) لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ نَصْرَهُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُمْ جُنْدٌ
مُحْضَرُونَ they cannot help them although they are for them an

218
